summary: Keep the WTS top-pool candidate detail contract reviewable across the Workbench BFF and React recruiter UI.
rationale: The top-pool detail API changes the generated Agent Workbench candidate summary contract. The PR must update the BFF projection, generated schema, React candidate summary compatibility code, fixtures, and visual baselines together so Workbench Contract CI can validate the branch against its own parent.
verification: PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 uv run --group dev python -m pytest tests/test_workbench_static_frontend.py tests/test_react_workbench_cutover_gate.py -q -p no:cacheprovider
PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 uv run --group dev python -m pytest tests/test_agent_workbench_contract.py -q -p no:cacheprovider
CI=1 pnpm --dir apps/web-react exec vitest run src/lib/stream/agentStream.test.ts src/lib/stream/agentStreamReducer.test.ts src/lib/stream/agentStreamView.test.ts --run
uv run --group dev python tools/check_pr_governance.py --base codex/wts-07-confirm-route
deletion_targets: A BFF top-pool detail PR whose OpenAPI output only becomes valid after a later frontend schema-sync PR
Candidate summary React components still reading the removed sourceKind field after the generated contract moves to sourceKinds
Workbench visual fixtures that no longer reflect recruiter-visible top-pool detail availability and evidence state
risks: The Workbench BFF must not expose raw provider payloads or full resumes through the top-pool summary contract.
React candidate cards must render the new sourceKinds, detailAvailability, accessState, and evidenceLevel fields without reintroducing developer/debug data.
Generated schema and visual baselines must stay deterministic so the branch remains independently reviewable in the stack.
